wordplay_note: The dead twin who speaks from inside his sister's body
card_description: Bill (in Edie) is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Dr. Bloodmoney*. Edie's dead twin brother who exists as a consciousness within her body. Name note: The dead twin who speaks from inside his sister's body. Death that is not death — consciousness persisting in impossible forms. First appears in *Dr. Bloodmoney*. Context: "Dick continues writing to Claudia, further elaborating on the spiritual significance of the rat's death."

linked_segments.summary: Dick continues writing to Claudia, further elaborating on the spiritual significance of the rat's death. He connects this act to broader cosmological ideas from Empedocles about the necessity of strif
linked_segments.title: 1975-02-27_Claudia_02.txt

reference.brief: Edie's dead twin brother who exists as a consciousness within her body
reference.significance: Death that is not death — consciousness persisting in impossible forms
